description_long: This object is a robust, floor-standing control cabinet designed for urban infrastructure, featuring a wide, angular body with a recessed central workspace. The front section houses a control panel with multiple round buttons and a prominent display screen set into a raised console. The side panels are thick and solid, providing structural support and likely housing internal wiring or equipment. The workspace beneath the control panel is open, allowing for legroom or access to the underside for maintenance. The exterior is finished in a dark, matte color, giving it a utilitarian and industrial appearance. There are no visible handles, locks, or ventilation grilles, and the cabinet appears to be designed for stationary use in public or semi-public environments.
description: A large, angular, floor-standing control cabinet with a central control panel, display screen, and solid, matte-finished exterior. The design is robust and industrial, suitable for urban infrastructure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the control panel with multiple buttons, a central display screen, and an open workspace beneath.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the cabinet, the angled control panel, and the solid, blocky construction.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, featureless back panel with no visible access points or details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring the other side, highlighting the thickness of the side panels and the angled control console.
